rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee in component type annexes; use assign only in implementation annexes for output bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model scope—no invented or example-based names.
generic_rules: Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Declare constants and equations only when they reflect concrete, model-supplied values or relations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use annex agree {** ... **}; for all AGREE content; format assume/guarantee/assign with quoted labels and semicolons.
Constants and equations must declare type and use exact value syntax: const name : type = value; eq name : type = expression;
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Never place guarantee statements inside implementation annexes—use assign instead for implementation bindings.
